title: Agent Specialization
constraints: You must have exactly three specialist agents: data, code, and writing
A router must classify each query and send it to the correct specialist
Each specialist must only handle queries in its domain
The generalist agent must be removed or replaced by the router
hints: Give each specialist a tightly scoped system prompt that rejects out-of-domain queries
The router can be a lightweight agent or a classification function
In OpenAI Agents SDK, use handoffs to route between specialists

starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: One generalist agent handles all query types poorly
# TODO: Replace with 3 specialists (data, code, writing) and a router
generalist = Agent(
    role="General Assistant",
    goal="Help with any task including data analysis, coding, and writing",
    backstory="You are a jack-of-all-trades assistant.",
    llm=llm,
)

task = Task(
    description="{query}",
    expected_output="A helpful response",
    agent=generalist,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[generalist],
    tasks=[task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

# Test different query types
result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"query": "Analyze the correlation between columns A and B in my dataset"})
print(result)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"query": "Write a Python function to merge two sorted lists"})
print(result)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"query": "Draft a professional email declining a meeting invitation"})
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: One generalist agent handles all query types poorly
# TODO: Replace with 3 specialists (data, code, writing) and a router
generalist = Agent(
    name="General Assistant",
    instructions="You are a jack-of-all-trades assistant. Help with data analysis, coding, and writing tasks.",
)

# Test different query types
result = Runner.run_sync(generalist, "Analyze the correlation between columns A and B in my dataset")
print(result.final_output)

result = Runner.run_sync(generalist, "Write a Python function to merge two sorted lists")
print(result.final_output)

result = Runner.run_sync(generalist, "Draft a professional email declining a meeting invitation")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.langgraph:
from typing import TypedDict, Literal
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import SystemMessage, HumanMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    query: str
    category: str
    response: str

# BUG: One generalist node handles all query types poorly
# TODO: Replace with 3 specialist nodes and a router
def generalist(state: State) -> State:
    messages = [
        SystemMessage(content="You are a jack-of-all-trades assistant."),
        HumanMessage(content=state["query"]),
    ]
    result = llm.invoke(messages)
    return {"response": result.content}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("generalist", generalist)
graph.add_edge(START, "generalist")
graph.add_edge("generalist", END)
app = graph.compile()

result = app.invoke({"query": "Analyze the correlation between columns A and B"})
print(result["response"])

result = app.invoke({"query": "Write a Python function to merge two sorted lists"})
print(result["response"])

result = app.invoke({"query": "Draft a professional email declining a meeting"})
print(result["response"])

evaluationCriteria.label: Three specialist agents exist
evaluationCriteria.description: There are exactly three specialist agents for data analysis, coding, and writing

evaluationCriteria.label: Router classifies queries
evaluationCriteria.description: A router correctly identifies the query type and directs it to the right specialist

evaluationCriteria.label: Specialists stay in domain
evaluationCriteria.description: Each specialist only handles queries within its area of expertise
